title: Error when building multidimensional signal with OPTFLAT problem
body:
---


Author Name: **Andrew Bardsley**
Original Redmine Issue: 872 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2015-01-21
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

The enclosed example file's generated C++ will not build.

The file contains a signal with two unpacked dimensions and a series of assignments between those elements.  With the command:

@> verilator -Wno-fatal --cc test.v@

This output is generated:

> %Warning-UNOPTFLAT: test.v:6: Signal unoptimizable: Feedback to clock or circular logic: v.some_signal
@> %Warning-UNOPTFLAT: Use "/* verilator lint_off UNOPTFLAT */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
> %Warning-UNOPTFLAT:      Example path: test.v:6:  v.some_signal
> %Warning-UNOPTFLAT:      Example path: test.v:9:  ASSIGNW
> %Warning-UNOPTFLAT:      Example path: test.v:6:  v.some_signal
@
The generated C++ for the signal has the two unpacked dimensions required but the generated  Vtest::_change_request
function only descends one dimension into the signal.

With the compilation command:

@> make -C obj_dir -f Vtest.mk@

Fails to compile with the error (one of a few of the same form):

@> ...
> Vtest.cpp:166:43: error: cast from ‘CData* {aka unsigned char*}’ to ‘IData {aka unsigned int}’ loses precision [-fpermissive]
>   = (IData)(vlTOPp->v__DOT__some_signal)[1U];
>                                            ^
> Vtest.cpp:166:2: error: incompatible types in assignment of ‘IData {aka unsigned int}’ to ‘CData [2] {aka unsigned char [2]}’
>   = (IData)(vlTOPp->v__DOT__some_signal)[1U];
>   ^
> ...@

My understanding is that such a function needs to descend through all the dimensions of the signal (or warn that it's not possible).

@> verilator --version ; g++ -v@

Yields:

@> Verilator 3.868 2014-12-20 rev verilator_3_866-36-g1a3378e
> ...
> gcc version 4.8.2 (Ubuntu 4.8.2-19ubuntu1)
@
I'm running on Ubuntu 14.04.

Thanks in advance
